description_long: This object is a large, tripod-mounted machine gun with a long, cylindrical barrel extending from a robust central receiver. The receiver is angular and blocky, featuring various mechanical details and attachment points, and is finished in a matte, two-tone color scheme of tan and black. The weapon is supported by three sturdy, splayed legs with wide, flat feet for stability, suggesting it is designed for stationary use in defensive or support roles. The barrel is smooth and unadorned, lacking visible cooling fins or suppressors, and there is no visible stock or shoulder support, emphasizing its emplacement nature. The feed system is not clearly visible, but the receiver has a boxy protrusion that may house internal mechanisms. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed mechanical parts and a rugged, military appearance suitable for urban deployment.
description: A large, tripod-mounted machine gun with a long, smooth barrel and a robust, angular receiver in tan and black. It features three wide-set legs for stability and a utilitarian, military design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, the angular receiver, and two of the three tripod legs.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the muzzle, the symmetrical spread of all three tripod legs, and the central alignment of the receiver.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the barrel, receiver, and the third tripod leg, mirroring the features seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the receiver and the converging tripod legs, with no visible stock or feed system.
